title: Applications, Productivity, Remote Access, Scripting, and AI-Assisted Support · Service Transfer
summary: A different user, device, environment, symptom, constraint, and changed condition requires an independent transfer of applications, productivity, remote access, scripting, and ai-assisted support into a new application support and bounded automation evidence pack. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of application support and bounded automation evidence pack with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Validate authoritative source, signature, license, architecture, OS, hardware, dependency, disk, network, account, data format, accessibility, compatibility, and rollback before install.
Preserve content, formulas, fonts, links, metadata, permissions, comments, accessibility, automation, print, and changed-client behavior across productivity formats.
Select attended and unattended remote methods with identity, consent, scope, encryption, least privilege, recording, clipboard, file transfer, disconnect, and local fallback controls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Application Readiness
steps.instruction:
lab case — A team installs apps, remotes into endpoints, runs scripts, and trusts AI suggestions without provenance, consent, secrets, tests, or rollback. The lab variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Application Readiness.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate authoritative source, signature, license, architecture, os, hardware, dependency, disk, network, account, data format, accessibility, compatibility, and rollback before install. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Application Readiness, keep Application Readiness connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A team installs apps, remotes into endpoints, runs scripts, and trusts AI suggestions without provenance, consent, secrets, tests, or rollback. The lab variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Application Readiness condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 2 220-1202 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 2 operating-system, security, software-troubleshooting, and operational-procedure objective and weighting.

options.text: Keep the plausible Ai Support Boundary output and hide the violated application support and bounded automation evidence pack assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Ai Support Boundary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use AI for bounded drafting or hypothesis generation while protecting data, validating sources and outputs, resisting injection, preserving human authorization, and documenting non-claims.
